title: Loca Luna Holds Soft Openings After Near-Complete Renovation of Riverdale Space

seo.title: Loca Luna Soft Openings Signal Riverdale Return After Remodel | Metro Little Rock News
seo.description: Loca Luna is soft-opening in Riverdale after a near-complete interior and exterior renovation, with the official reopen date contingent on invitation-only results.

summary: Loca Luna at 3519 Old Cantrell Road in Riverdale is running invitation-only soft opening lunches and dinners this week after a near-complete renovation of its interior and exterior. The Remolinos Group, which acquired the restaurant from longtime owner Mark Abernathy in August 2024, closed the space in September 2025 for the overhaul. The official public reopening date has not been announced and will depend on how the soft openings perform.

body_sections.body: Loca Luna closed in September 2025 for renovations and is now in its final stage before a public reopening. The Remolinos Group — which took ownership from Mark Abernathy, who ran the restaurant for decades as one of Little Rock's more established dining institutions — is holding invitation-only soft opening lunches and dinners this week at 3519 Old Cantrell Road. The official reopening date will be set based on those results.

body_sections.heading: What Changed
body_sections.body: The renovation was described as near-complete on both the interior and exterior. Outside, the facade has been upgraded and a fenced-in front patio was added — a tangible improvement for a street like Old Cantrell where outdoor seating matters during spring and fall. Interior changes were not released in full, but the scope of the project suggests a substantial reworking rather than a cosmetic refresh. The Remolinos Group also owns Red Door, which closed April 1 for its own extensive renovation and is expected to reopen with a new concept in summer 2026.

body_sections.heading: Riverdale Context
body_sections.body: Loca Luna has been one of the Old Cantrell corridor's dinner anchors for years, with a long-running identity tied to Abernathy's ownership. A change in ownership followed by a nine-month closure and full renovation is a meaningful transition — the restaurant that reopens will share a name and address but be a materially different operation than the one that closed. How much of the original identity the Remolinos Group carries forward versus resets is the open question for regulars.

impact_note: Keeping an anchor restaurant in place at 3519 Old Cantrell is better for the corridor than losing the space. The new patio is the most immediately useful change for neighbors — outdoor seating on that block in good weather has been a gap. The larger variable is how the Remolinos Group positions the food and service relative to what regulars remember, which soft openings are specifically designed to stress-test before the public finds out.
